title: button color is wrong
description: change calculate emi button to pink
ai_remark: [UNVERIFIED — human review required] Fix saved to output/fixed_1145c32c_emi_calculator.html. BUGS: The reported bug is that the 'Calculate EMI' button color is wrong and needs to be changed to pink. The current code sets the background color of all buttons to #3498db. Additionally, the script does not handle cases where the user enters negative numbers or zero for loan amount, interest rate, or duration. | UI/UX: Added a specific class for the 'Calculate EMI' button to change its color to pink. Also, added input validation to handle cases where the user enters negative numbers or zero for loan amount, interest rate, or duration. Changed the font size of the result label and span to improve readability.

title: Uncaught TypeError
description: When a user clicks on the 'Maintenance Dashboard' button, the code tries to navigate to '/dashboard' which may not exist, resulting in a 404 error.

title: Division by Zero Error
description: When the interest rate is 0, the code will throw a division by zero error because it attempts to calculate Math.pow(1 + R, N) with R being 0.

title: EMI Calculation Formula
description: The formula used to calculate the EMI is incorrect. The correct formula for calculating EMI is: EMI = (P * R * (1 + R)^N) / ((1 + R)^N - 1) when R and N are monthly values, but in this code R is divided by 12 and 100, so N should be the number of months and R should be the monthly interest rate.

title: Input Validation
description: The code checks if the input values are NaN, but it does not check if the values are negative or zero, which could also result in incorrect calculations.

title: UI Issue
description: When the user clicks on the 'Calculate EMI' button, the result is displayed below the button, but the page does not scroll to the result, so the user may not see the result if the page is not long enough.

title: Potential Overflow Error
description: The code uses Math.pow to calculate the power of a number. For large input values, this could result in a number that exceeds the maximum limit of JavaScript numbers, resulting in Infinity or NaN.

title: Inadequate Error Handling
description: The code only checks if the input values are NaN, but it does not handle other potential errors such as overflow errors or division by zero errors.
